skill.name: file-search
skill.description: Perform fast file name and content searches with ripgrep (rg); use it when you need to locate files by glob/regex, find keywords across directories, or replace common find/grep workflows.
skill.summary: Fast file search using ripgrep. Locate files by glob or regex patterns across any directory.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 47 static findings evaluated as false positives. The 5 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings match metadata and comments with no cryptographic code present. 32 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are markdown code examples showing rg command syntax, not executable shell scripts. 6 'System reconnaissance' findings flag the legitimate file-search purpose of the skill. 2 'Hardcoded URL' findings reference the project's own GitHub source repository for attribution. The skill is documentation-only with a benign Python validation script (shutil.which check). Risk is limited to external_commands when an AI tool executes the documented rg commands, which are read-only file search operations.

content.user_title: Search files and code with ripgrep
content.value_statement: Finding files or code patterns across large projects is slow with traditional tools. This skill uses ripgrep to perform fast file name and content searches, replacing slow find and grep pipelines with one high-performance command.
actual_capabilities: List all files matching a glob pattern in a directory using rg --files --glob
Search file contents with regex patterns across entire project trees
Display context lines around each match for quick code inspection
Filter searches by file type (e.g., --type rust, --type python) to reduce noise
Check if ripgrep is installed before running any search commands
Replace traditional find and grep pipelines with a single rg command
limitations: Cannot search binary file contents or non-text formats
Requires ripgrep (rg) to be installed on the system
Does not perform semantic or meaning-based search, only pattern matching
Search speed depends on filesystem performance and index availability

use_cases.title: Find configuration files across a project
use_cases.description: A developer needs to locate all YAML or JSON configuration files in a large project with many subdirectories. The skill uses rg --files with glob patterns to list matching files instantly.

use_cases.title: Search code for deprecated API usage
use_cases.description: A team lead wants to find all occurrences of a deprecated function call before a migration. The skill performs a regex content search with context lines to show how each call is used.

use_cases.title: Audit a repository for TODOs and FIXMEs
use_cases.description: A project manager wants to catalog all incomplete work items before a release. The skill searches for TODO and FIXME patterns with type filtering to scope results to source files only.

prompt_templates.title: List files by pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the file-search skill to find all Markdown files in the current project directory. List the matching files with their full paths.
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic file discovery for documentation review

prompt_templates.title: Search content with keyword
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the src/ directory for the keyword 'deprecated_api_v2'. Show 3 lines of context around each match so I can see how the function is being called.
prompt_templates.scenario: Finding specific code patterns before a refactor

prompt_templates.title: Type-filtered search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for all Python import statements using 'from' across the project. Restrict the search to Python files only using type filtering.
prompt_templates.scenario: Auditing dependencies in a Python project

prompt_templates.title: Multi-pattern regex search with output file
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the entire project for any of these patterns: 'HARDCODED_KEY', 'PASSWORD', 'SECRET'. Use case-insensitive matching. Write the results to a file named audit_result.md with the count of matches per pattern.
prompt_templates.scenario: Security audit for hardcoded secrets in source code

output_examples.input: List all Rust files in the project matching *.rs
Find all occurrences of 'fn main' in Rust files with 3 lines of context
output: Validation summary: PASS - 1 match found in 1 file

best_practices: Always run rg --version first to confirm ripgrep is installed before starting complex searches.
Use --type filters to narrow searches to relevant file types and reduce false positive matches.
Include context lines (-C) when searching code to understand how each match is used in context.
anti_patterns: Avoid searching the entire filesystem (/) when a project subdirectory would suffice and return faster results.
Do not run searches without specifying a path or glob pattern, as this may return unexpected results.
Do not use overly broad regex patterns that match nearly every line, which slows down review.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need ripgrep (rg) version 13.0.0 or higher installed on your system. Run rg --version to check.

faq.question: Can this skill search inside binary files?
faq.answer: No, ripgrep searches text content only. Binary files are skipped by default.

faq.question: How is ripgrep different from regular grep?
faq.answer: Ripgrep is much faster than grep for large codebases. It respects .gitignore rules by default and supports recursive search out of the box.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with file types not in the rg type list?
faq.answer: Yes, use --glob patterns instead of --type to match files by custom extensions or name patterns.

faq.question: Does this skill modify any files?
faq.answer: No. File search is read-only. It only locates and displays file names and matching content lines.

faq.question: What operating systems support ripgrep?
faq.answer: Ripgrep runs on Linux, macOS, and Windows. Installation instructions are available on the ripgrep GitHub page.
